_metadata.description: Secret scanner for Slack, Jira, Confluence, Asana, Wrike, Zendesk, Linear, GitHub, GitLab, and local filesystems.

cli.description: Run n0s1 as a command-line tool. Each tool maps to a subcommand.

docker.description: Run n0s1 via the official Docker image. Parameters are identical to the CLI — only the invocation prefix differs. No install required.
docker.example: docker run spark1security/n0s1 jira_scan --server https://myco.atlassian.net --email user@example.com --api-key TOKEN
docker.file_mounts: Use -v $(pwd)/file:/file to pass local regex, config, map, or report files into the container.
docker.when_to_use: Prefer Docker when no Python environment is available, in containerized CI (GitLab CI, Jenkins), or when a pinned reproducible version is needed.

python_sdk.description: Use n0s1 as a Python library via the SecretScanner class from n0s1.scanner.
python_sdk.return_value: dict with keys: 'findings' (list of leak dicts), 'scan_date', and metadata.

github_actions.description: Use n0s1 as a GitHub Actions step. Inputs map to CLI flags (see action repo for full schema).
github_actions.usage_pattern:
uses: spark1security/n0s1-action@main
with:
  scan-target: jira_scan
  password-key: ${{ secrets.JIRA_TOKEN }}
github_actions.key_difference: CLI --api-key becomes 'password-key' input; CLI --server becomes 'platform-url' input.

tools.name: n0s1_local_scan
tools.description:
Scan a local file or folder for leaked secrets using regex patterns.

CLI usage:
  n0s1 local_scan --path ./src

Python SDK usage:
  from n0s1 import scanner
  result = scanner.SecretScanner(target='local_scan', scan_path='./src').scan()
  # result['findings'] contains the list of detected leaks

path.description: Path to the local file or folder to be scanned. Maps to CLI --path and SDK scan_path parameter.

regex_file.description: Path to a custom .yaml or .toml file with regex patterns to match against.

config_file.description: Path to a YAML configuration file.

report_file.description: Output file path where the leak report will be saved.

show_matched_secret_on_logs.description: If true, shows the actual leaked secret in reports and logs. Use with caution.

debug.description: Enable debug mode. May further expose sensitive data.

limit.description: Maximum number of pages to return per HTTP request.

map.description: Enable mapping mode. Defines how many levels deep to map the scan scope. Output is saved as a JSON map file.

map_file.description: Path to a map file (e.g. n0s1_map.json) to customize the scan scope.

scope.description: Define a chunk of the map file to scan. Example: '3/4' scans the third quarter.

tools.name: n0s1_slack_scan
tools.description:
Scan Slack messages for leaked secrets.

CLI usage:
  n0s1 slack_scan --api-key SLACK_TOKEN

Python SDK usage:
  from n0s1 import scanner
  result = scanner.SecretScanner(target='slack_scan', api_key='SLACK_TOKEN').scan()

api_key.description: Slack token with OAuth scopes: search:read, users:read, chat:write. See: https://api.slack.com/tutorials/tracks/getting-a-token

post_comment.description: If true, posts a warning comment on messages where a secret leak is detected.

skip_comment.description: If true, skips scanning message threads; only scans top-level messages.

secret_manager.description: Name of a secret manager tool to recommend in warning comments (e.g. 'HashiCorp Vault').

contact_help.description: Contact information to include in warning comments for remediation assistance.

label.description: Unique identifier added to comments so n0s1 can detect previously flagged leaks.

regex_file.description: Path to a custom .yaml or .toml file with regex patterns.

config_file.description: Path to a YAML configuration file.

report_file.description: Output file path where the leak report will be saved.

show_matched_secret_on_logs.description: If true, shows the actual leaked secret in reports and logs. Use with caution.

limit.description: Maximum number of pages to return per HTTP request.

map.description: Enable mapping mode. Defines how many levels deep to map the scan scope.

map_file.description: Path to a map file to customize the scan scope.

scope.description: Search query to filter the scan scope.

tools.name: n0s1_jira_scan
tools.description:
Scan Jira tickets (title, description, and comments) for leaked secrets.

CLI usage:
  n0s1 jira_scan --server https://myco.atlassian.net --email user@example.com --api-key TOKEN

Python SDK usage:
  from n0s1 import scanner
  result = scanner.SecretScanner(
      target='jira_scan',
      server='https://myco.atlassian.net',
      email='user@example.com',
      api_key='TOKEN',
      scope='jql:project=SEC'
  ).scan()
  findings = result['findings']

post_comment.description: If true, posts a warning comment on tickets where a secret leak is detected.

skip_comment.description: If true, skips scanning ticket comments; only scans title and description.

secret_manager.description: Name of a secret manager tool to recommend in warning comments.

contact_help.description: Contact information to include in warning comments.

label.description: Unique identifier added to comments so n0s1 can detect previously flagged leaks.

regex_file.description: Path to a custom .yaml or .toml file with regex patterns.

config_file.description: Path to a YAML configuration file.

report_file.description: Output file path where the leak report will be saved.

report_format.description: Format of the output report. 'sarif' and 'SARIF' are equivalent.

show_matched_secret_on_logs.description: If true, shows the actual leaked secret in reports and logs. Use with caution.

limit.description: Maximum number of pages to return per HTTP request.

scope.description: JQL query to filter which tickets to scan (e.g. 'jql:project=SEC' or 'jql:project=MAR OR project="Auto Service"').

map.description: Enable mapping mode. Defines how many levels deep to map the scan scope.

map_file.description: Path to a map file to customize the scan scope.

tools.name: n0s1_confluence_scan
tools.description:
Scan Confluence pages for leaked secrets.

CLI usage:
  n0s1 confluence_scan --server https://myco.atlassian.net --email user@example.com --api-key TOKEN

Python SDK usage:
  from n0s1 import scanner
  result = scanner.SecretScanner(
      target='confluence_scan',
      server='https://myco.atlassian.net',
      email='user@example.com',
      api_key='TOKEN',
      scope='cql:space=SEC and type=page'
  ).scan()

api_key.description: Confluence API key. Can reuse the Jira API key for Atlassian Cloud.

post_comment.description: If true, posts a warning comment on pages where a secret leak is detected.

skip_comment.description: If true, skips scanning page comments.

secret_manager.description: Name of a secret manager tool to recommend in warning comments.

contact_help.description: Contact information to include in warning comments.

label.description: Unique identifier added to comments so n0s1 can detect previously flagged leaks.

regex_file.description: Path to a custom .yaml or .toml file with regex patterns.

config_file.description: Path to a YAML configuration file.

report_file.description: Output file path where the leak report will be saved.

show_matched_secret_on_logs.description: If true, shows the actual leaked secret in reports and logs. Use with caution.

limit.description: Maximum number of pages to return per HTTP request.

scope.description: CQL query to filter which pages to scan (e.g. 'cql:space=SEC and type=page').

map_file.description: Path to a map file to customize the scan scope.

tools.name: n0s1_github_scan
tools.description:
Scan GitHub repositories for leaked secrets across branches and files.

CLI usage:
  n0s1 github_scan --owner myorg --repo myrepo --api-key TOKEN

Python SDK usage:
  from n0s1 import scanner
  result = scanner.SecretScanner(
      target='github_scan',
      owner='myorg',
      repo='myrepo',
      branch='main',
      api_key='TOKEN'
  ).scan()

api_key.description: GitHub personal access token or app token. See: https://docs.github.com/en/apps/creating-github-apps/authenticating-with-a-github-app/generating-a-user-access-token-for-a-github-app

owner.description: GitHub account owner or organization name (case-insensitive). If omitted, all accessible repos are scanned.

repo.description: Repository name without .git extension (case-insensitive). If omitted, all repos under the owner are scanned.

branch.description: Branch to scan. Accepts a single branch name or comma-separated list (e.g. 'main,develop'). If omitted, all accessible branches are scanned.

post_comment.description: If true, posts a warning comment where a secret leak is detected.

skip_comment.description: If true, skips scanning PR/commit comments.

secret_manager.description: Name of a secret manager tool to recommend in warning comments.

contact_help.description: Contact information to include in warning comments.

label.description: Unique identifier added to comments so n0s1 can detect previously flagged leaks.

regex_file.description: Path to a custom .yaml or .toml file with regex patterns.

config_file.description: Path to a YAML configuration file.

report_file.description: Output file path where the leak report will be saved.

show_matched_secret_on_logs.description: If true, shows the actual leaked secret in reports and logs. Use with caution.

limit.description: Maximum number of pages to return per HTTP request.

scope.description: Search query to filter repos (e.g. 'search:org:myorg action in:name').

map.description: Enable mapping mode. Defines how many levels deep to map the scan scope.

map_file.description: Path to a map file to customize the scan scope.

tools.name: n0s1_gitlab_scan
tools.description:
Scan GitLab repositories for leaked secrets.

CLI usage:
  n0s1 gitlab_scan --server https://gitlab.com --owner mygroup --api-key TOKEN

Python SDK usage:
  from n0s1 import scanner
  result = scanner.SecretScanner(
      target='gitlab_scan',
      server='https://gitlab.com',
      owner='mygroup',
      api_key='TOKEN',
      scope='search:myproject'
  ).scan()

server.description: GitLab instance URL. Defaults to https://gitlab.com.

owner.description: GitLab group to scan. If omitted, all accessible projects are scanned.

repo.description: GitLab project ID or path with namespace (e.g. 'mygroup/myproject'). If omitted, all projects are scanned.

branch.description: Branch to scan. If omitted, all accessible branches are scanned.

post_comment.description: If true, posts a warning comment where a secret leak is detected.

secret_manager.description: Name of a secret manager tool to recommend in warning comments.

contact_help.description: Contact information to include in warning comments.

label.description: Unique identifier added to comments so n0s1 can detect previously flagged leaks.

regex_file.description: Path to a custom .yaml or .toml file with regex patterns.

config_file.description: Path to a YAML configuration file.

report_file.description: Output file path where the leak report will be saved.

show_matched_secret_on_logs.description: If true, shows the actual leaked secret in reports and logs. Use with caution.

limit.description: Maximum number of pages to return per HTTP request.

scope.description: Search query to filter the scan scope (e.g. 'search:myproject').

map.description: Enable mapping mode. Defines how many levels deep to map the scan scope.

map_file.description: Path to a map file to customize the scan scope.
